case_title: WILLIAM BECKER VS. BRINKS, INC ET AL

docket_entries.description: MASTER CALENDAR MOTION ON JUL-08-2015 IN DEPT. 206, HEARING ON APPLICANT/PLAINTIFF WILLIAM BECKER'S WAIVER OF COURT FEES AND COSTS. ATTORNEY DONALD EDWARD BLOOM APPEARING FOR PLAINTIFF. NO APPEARANCE BY APPLICANT. THE COURT DENIED THE APPLICATION IN WHOLE WITH A DEFERRED PAYMENT THAT IS DUE BY 1/8/16. ORDER SIGNED, AND ENDORSED-FILED COPY MAILED TO APPLICANT. JUDGE: JOHN K. STEWART; CLERK: R. VELUZ; MATTER NOT REPORTED. (206)

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT BRINKS, INC.'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. THE CAUSE IS ARGUED AND THE COURT CONFIRMS THE TENTATIVE RULING WITH MODIFICATION. THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION FOR BATTERY IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF WILLIAM BECKER CITES NO AUTHORITY, AND THE COURT IS AWARE OF NONE, THAT SOUND WAVES ARE SUFFICIENT CONTACT WITH A PERSON TO CONSTITUTE THE TORT OF BATTERY. IF HE CAN DO SO IN GOOD FAITH, PLAINTIFF IS GRANTED LEAVE TO ALLEGE A CLAIM FOR INTENTIONAL INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS. COURT GRANTS TWO WEEKS LEAVE TO FILE A SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT. WRITTEN ORDER IS SIGNED AND FILED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: JOSE RIOS MERIDA; REPORTER: SHERRY L. SAWYER, CSR NO. 5976

LAW AND MOTION, 302, DEFENDANT BRINKS, INC.'S DEMURRER TO THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION FOR INTENTIONAL INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS IN THE SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED. THE SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT ADEQUATELY ALLEGES EXTREME AND OUTRAGEOUS CONDUCT BY ALLEGING THAT MR. SPARKS, WITH THE INTENT TO INJURE PLAINTIFF WILLIAM BECKER, INTENTIONALLY RAN THE ENGINE OF HIS TRUCK "TO ACHIEVE A TOTALLY DEAFENING AND OVERWHELMING NOISE." ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR #5976 =302/HK
MINI MINUTES FOR JUL-18-2017 10:30 AM FOR DEPT 610

MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-16-2016 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. DONALD BLOOM, TEL. NO. 1-415-282-9955 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. RYAN SANDERS OF LEACH & MCGREVY LLP, TEL. NO. 1-415-775-4455 EXT. 309 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT BRINKS BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Defendant Brinks, Inc.'s motion to strike the punitive damages allegations in the second amended complaint is denied. The second amended complaint adequately alleges extreme malicious and oppressive conduct by alleging that Mr. Sparks, with the intent to injure plaintiff William Becker, intentionally ran the engine of his truck "to achieve a totally deafening and overwhelming noise." Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976 =302/hk

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-16-2016 Overruled
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. DONALD BLOOM, TEL. NO. 1-415-282-9955 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. RYAN SANDERS OF LEACH & MCGREVY LLP, TEL. NO. 1-415-775-4455 EXT. 309 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT BRINKS BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Defendant Brinks, Inc.'s demurrer to the second cause of action for intentional infliction of emotional distress in the second amended complaint is overruled. The second amended complaint adequately alleges extreme and outrageous conduct by alleging that Mr. Sparks, with the intent to injure plaintiff William Becker, intentionally ran the engine of his truck "to achieve a totally deafening and overwhelming noise." Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976 =302/hk

MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-07-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Donald E. Bloom, Esq. (415.282.9955) appears as counsel for plaintiff/opposing party

Attorney, Paul O'Keefe of Leach & McGreevy (415.775.4455) appears as counsel for defendant/demurring party

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANT BRINKS, INC.'S MOTION TO STRIKE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT, to strike the punitive damages allegations in the first amended complaint is off calendar as moot in light of the ruling on Brinks' demurrer to the battery claim.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Sherry L. Sawyer, CSR No. 5976, email: ssawyer@sftc.org. (D302)

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-07-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Donald E. Bloom, Esq. (415.282.9955) appears as counsel for plaintiff/opposing party

Attorney, Paul O'Keefe of Leach & McGreevy (415.775.4455) appears as counsel for defendant/demurring party

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter. The Court confirms the tentative ruling with modification.

DEFENDANT BRINKS, INC.'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT, as to the second cause of action for battery is SUSTAINED without leave to amend. Plaintiff William Becker cites no authority, and the court is aware of none, that sound waves are sufficient contact with a person to constitute the tort of battery. If he can do so in good faith, plaintiff is granted leave to allege a claim for intentional infliction of emotional distress. Court grants two weeks leave to file a second amended complaint. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Sherry L. Sawyer, CSR No. 5976, email: ssawyer@sftc.org. (D302)
